mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as equity prices reprice higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return is non-positive across all months with positive PAYEMS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.0% indicates restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return is positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production reveal a coordinated contraction in both demand and supply, amplifying recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM and INDPRO declines MoM
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal a recovering labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline returns when UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and PAYEMS is rising, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets price in near-term earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return is negative or zero across months with positive INDPRO growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand that precedes downward corporate earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of months when RSAFS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation measured by month-over-month CPI increases erodes real corporate earnings and pressures monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return is non-negative when CPIAUCSL is rising over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of months when UNRATE rises month-over-month.
